title: 第一部分 基础训练 TEST 1

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 A
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
École Morden Middle School invites an excellent teacher to fill the role of the Grade 7 French teacher for a term.

* **Pay:** $5,800 per month
* **Date:**
  * Applicants can apply for this position from June 3, 2024 to July 31, 2024.
  * Interviews will begin on August 5, 2024, and continue until the position is filled.
* **Description:**
  * A teacher who has the ability to teach French well will be considered first.
  * A teaching certificate is also needed.
  * He or she must have good written and spoken communication skills.
  * He or she must have rich teaching experience, work as part of a team and get on well with other teachers.
* **How to apply:**
  * Interested applicants are needed to visit our website at www.westernsd.mb.ca to fill out an application form.
  * You can call us during workdays or send us an e-mail at any time for more information.
  * **Telephone:** 204-822-4262
  * **E-mail:** hr@westernsd.mb.ca

questions.prompt: When will École Morden Middle School start its interviews?
options: A. On June 3, 2024.
B. On July 31, 2024.
C. On August 5, 2024.
D. On September 1, 2024.
questions.translation: 莫登中学什么时候开始面试？
questions.explanation: 根据文中“Interviews will begin on August 5, 2024”可知，面试将于2024年8月5日开始。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: The passage mentions the following EXCEPT ________.
options: A. the pay
B. the workplace
C. the students' French level
D. the closing date for application
questions.translation: 文章提到了以下内容，除了 ________。
questions.explanation: 文章提到了薪水（Pay: $5,800）、工作地点（École Morden Middle School）和申请截止日期（July 31, 2024），但没有提到学生的法语水平。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: Which of the following might be welcomed most?
options: A. Tom, who studied English and French at Harvard before.
B. Jack, who has good written and spoken communication skills.
C. Lidya, who has been a middle school headmaster for ten years.
D. Mary, who is good at teaching French and has a teaching certificate.
questions.translation: 以下哪个人可能最受欢迎？
questions.explanation: 根据“Description”部分提到的“A teacher who has the ability to teach French well will be considered first. A teaching certificate is also needed.”可知，擅长教法语并持有教师资格证的Mary最符合优先考虑的条件。因此答案为D。

questions.prompt: Interested applicants must ________ to apply for the position.
options: A. go to the school in person
B. make a phone call at 204-822-4262
C. send an e-mail at hr@westernsd.mb.ca
D. complete the application form online
questions.translation: 有意向的申请人必须 ________ 来申请这个职位。
questions.explanation: 根据“How to apply”部分“Interested applicants are needed to visit our website ... to fill out an application form”可知，需要到网站上填写申请表，即在线完成申请表。因此答案为D。

questions.prompt: What is the passage?
options: A. A storybook.
B. A poster.
C. A novel.
D. A guidebook.
questions.translation: 这篇文章是什么？
questions.explanation: 这是一篇招聘广告海报（poster），邀请老师来申请职位。因此答案为B。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 B
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Scooter is a seven-year-old dog. He is black. His body has no hair, but he has some thin white hair on his head. His back legs face backward, and his tongue is always out of his mouth. Scooter's special looks won him the 2023 World's Ugliest Dog contest on June 23.

The World's Ugliest Dog contest started in the 1970s. The event is different from most dog contests. This contest is meant to show that every dog needs love and a warm home.

Scooter may be a star now, but when he was a puppy, his life was at risk. He was born with his back legs facing backward, making them useless for walking. However, Scooter learned to walk with his two front legs. He can even jump to climb stairs. But walking on his front legs makes him tired. So he has to take a break often. Luckily, someone made Scooter a special tool that makes his life much easier—a cart with wheels that helps him get around quickly.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is TRUE about Scooter?
options: A. He has much hair on his body.
B. His front legs face backward.
C. His tongue is always out of his mouth.
D. He has some white hair on his back legs.
questions.translation: 关于Scooter，以下哪项是正确的？
questions.explanation: 根据第一段“...and his tongue is always out of his mouth.”可知，它的舌头总是伸在嘴外面。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: The purpose of the World's Ugliest Dog contest is to ________.
options: A. show how amazing dogs can be
B. show every dog needs a loving home
C. give every dog a chance to be beautiful
D. share how fun it is to be friends with dogs
questions.translation: 世界最丑狗狗大赛的目的是 ________。
questions.explanation: 根据第二段“This contest is meant to show that every dog needs love and a warm home.”可知，该竞赛旨在表明每只狗都需要爱和一个温暖的家。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: What can help Scooter move around fast?
options: A. His back legs.
B. His special cart.
C. His front legs.
D. His special tail.
questions.translation: 什么能帮助Scooter快速四处活动？
questions.explanation: 根据最后一段“...a cart with wheels that helps him get around quickly.”可知，是一个带轮子的特制手推车帮助它快速移动。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: We can read the passage from the ________ part of a newspaper.
options: A. animal
B. health
C. science
D. travel
questions.translation: 我们可以在报纸的 ________ 版块读到这篇文章。
questions.explanation: 整篇文章都在讲述一只特殊的狗狗Scooter赢得最丑狗狗大赛以及它的生活经历，因此属于“动物（animal）”版块。因此答案为A。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 C
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
A report shows that Japan has the lowest rate of childhood obesity among the developed countries. Experts say there are different factors, including a health-conscious society and regular check-ups for children. A nationwide school lunch program is believed to have played a key role.

The lunches are provided for all primary schools and most middle schools in Japan. A team of experts create the menus. They try to achieve a balance between carbohydrates, meat or fish, and vegetables. Each meal has around 600-700 calories. "School lunches provide nutrition that meals at home are usually short of," said an official. "It helps children to get the nutritional balance."

Japanese school lunches are usually served in the classroom. Students often give out food to each other and clean up the room after lunch. There is also a daily broadcast at school to explain the nutrition in the lunch of the day. This is a good way to educate kids about health.

The Japanese government studies nutrition and eating habits in the country every year. The results are useful in deciding what goes into the school meals. Of course, up to now, Japan has not completely made obese children disappear.

questions.prompt:
What make Japan have the lowest rate of childhood obesity among the developed countries?
① A health-conscious society.
② Regular check-ups for children.
③ A nationwide school lunch program.
④ Developed medical skills.
options: A. ①②③
B. ②③④
C. ①③④
D. ①②④
questions.translation:
是什么让日本在发达国家中儿童肥胖率最低？
①注重健康的社会。
②儿童定期体检。
③全国性的学校午餐计划。
④发达的医疗技术。
questions.explanation: 根据第一段“...different factors, including a health-conscious society and regular check-ups for children. A nationwide school lunch program is believed to have played a key role.”可知，包括注重健康的社会、定期体检和全国午餐计划，而没有提到发达的医疗技术。因此答案为A。

questions.prompt: What does the underlined word "factors" mean in Chinese?
options: A. 地位
B. 因素
C. 想法
D. 影响
questions.translation: 画线单词“factors”的中文意思是什么？
questions.explanation: 第一段“Experts say there are different factors...”中，factor意为“因素”，后文列举了健康意识社会、体检、午餐计划等因素。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: What does the third paragraph mainly tell us?
options: A. The lunches are provided for all middle schools in Japan.
B. Japanese students have more nutritious lunches.
C. Japanese parents are good at cooking delicious lunches.
D. How school lunches are served and how health education is integrated.
questions.translation: 第三段主要告诉了我们什么？
questions.explanation: 第三段讲述了午餐在教室里供应、学生分发食物和打扫卫生，以及学校通过每日广播讲解营养，指出“This is a good way to educate kids about health.（这是教育孩子健康的好方法）”，体现了日本如何通过学校午餐及相关活动帮助孩子建立健康/营养教育。因此答案为D。

questions.prompt: Where do primary school students usually have lunch in Japan?
options: A. In the dining rooms.
B. In their classrooms.
C. On the playgrounds.
D. At their homes.
questions.translation: 在日本，小学生通常在哪里吃午餐？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段第一句“Japanese school lunches are usually served in the classroom.”可知，通常在教室里用午餐。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT true?
options: A. There haven't been any obese students in Japan.
B. Students should eat more meat, fish and vegetables.
C. The Japanese government cares about the students very much.
D. We should learn from Japan in reducing childhood obesity rates.
questions.translation: 以下哪项是错误的？
questions.explanation: 根据第四段最后一句“Of course, up to now, Japan has not completely made obese children disappear.”可知，日本尚未完全让肥胖儿童消失，因此“日本没有肥胖学生”是不对的。答案为A。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 D
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给 of A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
74-year-old Mark Levine is from the US. Now, he works as an educator at the Minzu University of China. He has been living in China for 17 years.

Mark has been teaching English at the university for 15 years. About 50% of the students come from the 55 Chinese ethnic minorities. Mark says, "Through my students, I've learned some about their unusual traditions. They've taught me as much as I've taught them."

"When I look at my students, I see that students from different minorities study together. After class, they play together, shop together and eat together. It's completely different from what is described in western media. And what I see is a commonality," he adds. "Here, everyone thinks, 'Our lives are going to get better if we help make China better because we are China and China is us.'"

Mark's heart and home is in China. That has inspired his talent in music, driving him to write over 80 songs. Recently, he wrote a song called My China Surprise. In the song, he sings that "My China Surprise, you see it in my eyes. It's the light that shines in them wherever I go." Mark says he can always find new things that he is not expecting. That's one of the most exciting things about being in China.

questions.prompt: Mark Levine came to China when he was ________.
options: A. 50 years old
B. 57 years old
C. 59 years old
D. 74 years old
questions.translation: 马克·莱文来到中国时是多少岁？
questions.explanation: 根据第一段“74-year-old Mark Levine is from the US... He has been living in China for 17 years.”可知，他现在74岁，已经在中国生活了17年，因此他来到中国时是 74 - 17 = 57岁。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: What do Mark's words in the third paragraph show?
options: A. The students get on well with one another.
B. The students often compare with each other.
C. The students find it hard to understand one another.
D. The students have very similar habits and lifestyles.
questions.translation: 马克在第三段的话表明了什么？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段描述，不同少数民族的学生在一起学习、课后一起玩耍、购物和吃饭，这表明他们相处得非常好（get on well with one another）。因此答案为A。

questions.prompt: From the fourth paragraph, we can infer that Mark ________.
options: A. is good at singing Chinese songs
B. misses his home country a lot
C. is in deep love with China
D. has no interest in art
questions.translation: 从第四段中，我们可以推断出马克 ________。
questions.explanation: 第四段提到“Mark's heart and home is in China.”（马克的内心和家都在中国）并且他创作了80多首歌表达对中国的热爱，可推断出他深爱着中国。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
options: A. Mark and his students learn from each other.
B. Mark has taught music for more than 17 years.
C. It's hard for Mark to find new and interesting things.
D. More than 55% of Mark's students are ethnic minorities students.
questions.translation: 根据文章，以下哪项是正确的？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段“They've taught me as much as I've taught them.”可知，马克和他的学生互相学习。因此答案为A。

questions.prompt: What's the purpose of this passage?
options: A. To talk about what we should do to make our lives better.
B. To show how ethnic minorities students live in a university.
C. To tell us how the teachers of the Minzu University of China work.
D. To introduce a foreign teacher who wrote songs to express his love for China.
questions.translation: 这篇文章的目的是什么？
questions.explanation: 整篇文章介绍了一位在中央民族大学教书的外籍教师马克·莱文，讲述他在中国生活、与学生相处，并通过写歌表达他对中国的热爱。因此答案为D。

sections.title: 二、任务型阅读
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
We usually think of money as bills and coins. In fact, silk, bottle caps, and other things were used in history to pay for goods.

In early history, humans bartered without using money. For example, the ancient Egyptians swapped clothes for tools or wood. The first and oldest form of money was cows and goats. About 3,000 years ago, Chinese people began using shells as money. Shells were the most widely used form of money in history.

In the Qin Dynasty, the earliest copper coins were made by the Chinese. Silver coins first appeared in Lydia, and then they were introduced to Greece and Rome.

The first paper money appeared in China in the Northern Song Dynasty. Then Marco Polo came to China. When he returned home, he introduced the paper money to Europeans. Different kinds of paper money were made by hand and used throughout Europe over the next several centuries. It was not until 1661 that Sweden became the first European country to print paper money.

Now many people don't take paper money or coins with them, as they can pay for nearly anything with their phones.

questions.prompt: When did people begin to use shells as money?
options: A. About 3,000 years ago.
B. About 1,661 years ago.
C. About 6,000 years ago.
D. About 10,000 years ago.
questions.translation: 人们什么时候开始使用贝壳作为货币？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段“About 3,000 years ago, Chinese people began using shells as money.”可知，大约在3000年前开始使用。答案为A。

questions.prompt: Where were silver coins first used?
options: A. In China.
B. In Lydia.
C. In Greece.
D. In Rome.
questions.translation: 银币最早在哪里使用？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段“Silver coins first appeared in Lydia...”可知，最早在吕底亚出现和使用。答案为B。

questions.prompt: What did Marco Polo do after he returned from China?
options: A. He printed paper money in Sweden.
B. He travelled to America.
C. He introduced the paper money to Europeans.
D. He wrote a book about the Qin Dynasty.
questions.translation: 马可·波罗从中国回来后做了什么？
questions.explanation: 根据第四段“When he returned home, he introduced the paper money to Europeans.”可知，他把纸币介绍给了欧洲人。答案为C。

questions.prompt: Why don't many people take paper money or coins with them now?
options: A. Because they don't want to buy anything.
B. Because bills and coins are too heavy.
C. Because they prefer bartering without money.
D. Because they can pay for nearly anything with their phones.
questions.translation: 为什么现在很多人不随身携带纸币或硬币？
questions.explanation: 根据最后一段“...as they can pay for nearly anything with their phones.”可知，因为他们可以用手机支付几乎任何费用。答案为D。

questions.prompt: What does the passage mainly talk about?
options: A. The history of money.
B. The history of China.
C. How to pay with phones.
D. Why people barter.
questions.translation: 这篇文章主要讲了什么？
questions.explanation: 文章描述了货币从物物交换、贝壳、铜币/银币、纸币到现代移动支付的演变历史，即货币的历史。答案为A。
